q: A capacitor is an electrical device that stores electrical energy in the form of an electric field.
A capacitor consists of two conductors separated by a large distance.
The capacitor was originally known as a ___.
Which type of capacitors use plastic film as the dielectric medium?
Film capacitors are available in nearly any value and working voltages up to $1500\ V$.
In which circuits are ceramic capacitors typically used?
a: High-frequency circuits such as audio frequency ($af$) to radio frequency ($rf$)

q: Ceramic capacitors are mainly used for high stability performances with low ___.
The capacitance values of ceramic capacitors are stable with respect to applied voltage, frequency, and temperature.
What material is used as the dielectric in electrolytic capacitors?
The two main types of electrolytic capacitors are ___ and aluminum.
The failure of electrolytic capacitors can be hazardous, resulting in an explosion or fire.
Variable capacitors mostly use what as the dielectric medium?
The capacitance of a variable capacitor can be mechanically adjusted.
A supercapacitor (SC) is also called an ___.
Supercapacitors typically store $10$ to $100$ times more energy than electrolytic capacitors.
Which application uses supercapacitors for power backup?
Film capacitors and ceramic capacitors are polarized capacitors.
Non-polarized capacitors do not have ___ (positive and negative terminals).
What is one primary use of capacitors in power supplies?
Capacitors are used for blocking DC current after getting fully charged but allow the AC current to pass through it.
When a capacitor is connected to a battery, the two conductors have charges of equal magnitude and opposite signs.
The magnitude of the charge on each conductor, $Q$, is called the ___ of the capacitor.
The quantity of charge $Q$ on the capacitor is linearly proportional to what?
The capacitance of a capacitor is defined as the ratio of the charge to the potential difference of the capacitor.
The SI unit of capacitance is ___ (F).
One Farad is equivalent to $1\ C\ V^{-1}$.
The farad is named in honor of which scientist?
$1$ microfarad ($\mu F$) is equal to ___ $F$.
$1$ picofarad ($pF$) is equal to ___ $F$.
When the charge on the capacitor is increased, the capacitance of a capacitor remains constant.
Capacitance of a capacitor depends on which of the following?
a: The size and shape of the capacitor

q: The capacitance of a capacitor depends on the nature of the insulator between the two conductors.
The amount of charge given to a conductor to change its potential by one unit is called the ___ of the conductor.
Typical devices have capacitances ranging from microfarad ($\mu F$) to picofarad ($pF$).
If potential difference $V$ increases while $C$ is constant, what happens to $Q$?
The relationship for capacitance is represented as $C = \frac{Q}{V}$.
$1$ nanofarad ($nF$) = ___ $F$.
What happens to the potential difference when the charge on the capacitor is increased?
A capacitor is an electrical power source.
The potential difference $V$ between two conductors of the capacitor is called the potential difference of the ___.
A parallel-plate capacitor consists of two parallel metal plates separated by air or other insulating material.
Charging a capacitor means connecting the capacitor to a ___.
What is the formula for surface charge density $\sigma$ on each plate?
If the plates are very close together, the electric field between the plates is uniform.
The value of the electric field between the plates is expressed as $E = \frac{\sigma}{\epsilon}$, where $\epsilon$ is the ___ of the insulating material.
The magnitude of the potential difference $V$ between the plates equals $E d$.
The capacitance of a parallel-plate capacitor is directly proportional to what?
The capacitance of a parallel-plate capacitor is inversely proportional to the separation of the plates.
The permittivity of vacuum $\epsilon_0$ is equal to ___ $C^2\ N^{-1}m^{-2}$.
What is the dielectric constant $\kappa$ for vacuum?
For all insulating materials other than vacuum, the dielectric constant $\kappa > 1$.
The dielectric constant $\kappa$ is the ratio of its permittivity to that of ___.
The dielectric constant can be defined as the ratio of two capacitances: $\kappa = \frac{C}{C_0}$.
When a charged capacitor is disconnected from a battery and a dielectric is inserted, the potential difference:
Since the charge $Q$ of the capacitor does not change when disconnected, a decrease in $V$ indicates an increase in capacitance.
Dielectric constant can be expressed as the ratio of capacitances as well as the ratio of ___.
Which of the following is an advantage provided by a dielectric?
a: Possible mechanical support between the plates

q: Insulating materials have values of dielectric constant $\kappa$ greater than unity.
A dielectric increases the maximum operating voltage of a capacitor even though the physical ___ of the capacitor doesn't change.
Which material has a dielectric constant of 81 at $18\ ^{\circ}C$?
The permittivity $\epsilon$ of an insulating material is related to that of a vacuum $\epsilon_0$ as $\epsilon = \kappa \epsilon_0$.
The dielectric constant of air at 1 atm is ___.
Mica has a dielectric constant range of 3-6.
The dielectric strength of a dielectric is generally ___ than that of air.
Inserting a dielectric allows plates to be close together without electrical contact.
A capacitor stores electrical energy in the form of an electric field.
When the capacitor is charged, the process can be viewed as the apparent transfer of ___ between two conductors.
Where is work done during the charging of a capacitor?
a: In the transfer of charge from lower potential to higher potential

q: The magnitude of the charge on the two conductors increases gradually during the charging process.
The work done in charging the capacitor is given by the ___ area under the $Q-V$ curve.
The energy stored by the capacitor is equal to $\frac{1}{2}QV$.
Which of the following is NOT a correct expression for energy $W$?
Before a capacitor is charged, the potential difference between two conductors is zero.
The average potential difference of the capacitor before and after it is charged is denoted by ___.
What effect does a resistor have on the charging process of a capacitor?
a: It slows down the charging process

q: The greater the values of resistance and capacitance, the longer it takes to charge the capacitor.
The product of the resistance $R$ and the capacitance $C$ is called the ___ $\tau$.
The time constant $\tau$ is measured in volts.
During the charging process, capacitor voltage $V_C$ rises with time according to which factor?
The voltage provided by the power supply is denoted as $V_0$.
During discharging, the voltage of the capacitor falls off with time according to the equation $V_C = V_0(___)$.
What is required to connect with the capacitor in charging and discharging?
The energy stored in a capacitor can be analyzed from the $Q-V$ graph.
Energy of the capacitor $W$ is equal to $\frac{1}{2} ___$.
The time constant $\tau$ is measured in second.
There are two types of capacitor combinations: parallel and series.
In a parallel combination, all the capacitors have the same ___.
In a parallel combination, do the capacitors carry the same or different amounts of charges?
The equivalent capacitance of capacitors connected in parallel is the sum of the capacitances of the individual capacitors.
For $n$ identical capacitors connected in parallel, the equivalent capacitance $C_p$ is ___.
In a series combination, the negatively charged plate of one capacitor is connected to the positively charged plate of the other.
Which property is the same for all capacitors connected in series?
Capacitors connected in series have different potential differences.
The total potential difference of a series combination is $V = V_1 + V_2 + ___$.
For capacitors in series, the reciprocal of the equivalent capacitance is the ___ of the reciprocals of their individual capacitances.
The equivalent capacitance $C_s$ of $n$ identical capacitors in series is $C_s = \frac{C}{n}$.
In series, the magnitude of charge on the equivalent capacitor is ___ when total potential difference $V$ is applied.
The equivalent capacitance of a series combination is always greater than any of the individual capacitances.
In which connection of the capacitors is the potential difference of each capacitor the same?
In series connection, each capacitor has the same charge.
